acceptance_criteria.criterion: library.py agent remove deletes the agent's installed handler asset directory for every harness, leaving no orphaned handler files
library.py agent use detects a handler-only catalog change (declared handlers set changed, prompt file byte-identical) and triggers a reinstall through the normal use path

commits.message: test(CL-du1x): red - agent remove deletes handler dirs
feat(CL-du1x): green - remove agent handler dirs
test(CL-du1x): red - handler catalog change reinstalls
feat(CL-du1x): green - reinstall on handler catalog changes

tests.result: failed as expected before production code: handler directories were missing from dry-run delete paths
passed after remove_agent deleted and previewed handler roots
failed as expected before production code: the handler-only catalog change was skipped and new handlers were not installed
passed after cmd_use detected handler declaration drift

tests.command: rm -rf /private/tmp/cl-du1x-xdg && XDG_DATA_HOME=/private/tmp/cl-du1x-xdg UV_CACHE_DIR=/private/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_library_py_installers.py

downstream_impacts: agent remove now deletes the exact per-harness <agent-name>-handlers directory and previews that deletion in dry-run output.

agent use now refreshes already-installed agents when declared handler targets differ from lockfile-recorded handler targets; non-agent primitives and agents with no declared or recorded handlers remain unaffected.
